step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("evidence linking specialty care to survival improvements in congestive heart failure")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

After reviewing the retrieved evidence, I confirm that specialty care is strongly linked to improved survival in patients with congestive heart failure. Multiple sources cite structured interprofessional models—especially heart failure clinics—that involve cardiologists, nurses, pharmacists, and physical therapists collaborating to deliver continuous, guideline-driven care. One systematic review found that such interprofessional approaches reduced all-cause mortality by approximately 25%, demonstrating direct survival benefit. This finding provides robust empirical support for option A.
